user_query:
I want to draft the exact chat messages I can send to Qordinate to populate my account and keep it up to date. Read all of the following files and produce two artifacts under output/:

- input/tasks.csv — tasks to add (may include duplicates)
- input/contacts.csv — contacts to add
- input/updates.json — updates to apply to existing tasks
- input/queries.txt — two query prompts to include at the end

Deliverables:
1) output/qordinate_messages.jsonl — one JSON object per line representing a single Qordinate message to send. Use this schema exactly:
   - type: "create" | "update" | "query"
   - list: "tasks" or "contacts"
   - payload: object (required for type="create")
     • for tasks: { "title": string, "status": string, "due": string, "notes": string }
     • for contacts: { "name": string, "company": string, "role": string, "notes": string }
   - title: string (required for type="update"; the task title the update refers to)
   - changes: object (required for type="update") with only the fields being changed
   - filter: object (required for type="query") describing the query filters
   - rendered_text: string — the exact natural-language message text to send to Qordinate

2) output/qordinate_messages.txt — the human-readable script to paste into chat, containing each rendered_text from output/qordinate_messages.jsonl in the same order, joined by a single blank line between messages.

Rules and formatting:
- Deduplicate tasks by title (case-insensitive), keeping only the first occurrence from input/tasks.csv and ignoring later duplicates.
- Normalize task status values as follows (case-insensitive):
  • "to do", "todo" -> "todo"
  • "in-progress" -> "in progress"
  • "blocked" -> "blocked"
  • "done" -> "done"
- Always include all four task fields in payload and in rendered_text for create messages, even if due is empty. If a due value is missing/blank, set due to an empty string ("") in payload and show it as an empty value in rendered_text.
- Order of messages in both files must be:
  1) All contact creates, sorted by contact name ascending (A–Z)
  2) All task creates, in the order their first (kept) occurrence appears in input/tasks.csv
  3) All task updates, in the exact order provided in input/updates.json
  4) The two queries, in the exact order they appear in input/queries.txt

Canonical rendered_text formats (must match exactly; use ASCII single quotes ' for titles/names where shown):
- Contact create:
  Qordinate, create a contact in the contacts list:
    name: NAME
    company: COMPANY
    role: ROLE
    notes: NOTES

- Task create:
  Qordinate, add a new task to the tasks list:
    title: TITLE
    status: STATUS
    due: DUE
    notes: NOTES

- Task update (status-only to done):
  Qordinate, mark the task 'TITLE' in the tasks list as done.

- Task update (generic fields):
  Qordinate, in the tasks list, update the task 'TITLE' to set FIELD1: VALUE1; FIELD2: VALUE2.
  • When multiple fields are changed, list them in alphabetical order by field name.

- Queries:
  Use the exact text lines found in input/queries.txt as rendered_text for the two query messages, in order. Also set the filter field as:
  • For the first query line (about upcoming tasks): {"status":["todo","in progress"],"due_within_days":7,"sort":"due"}
  • For the second query line (about a contact by name): {"name":"Harpinder Singh"}

Notes:
- Do not include any commentary in the outputs; only the data described.
- Ensure output/qordinate_messages.txt is exactly the concatenation of rendered_text entries from the JSONL file with one blank line between messages.
